mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances or the t-statistic for return difference versus baseline is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation signals overheating demand that forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint high percentiles of RSAFS and CPIAUCSL yield positive 20-day returns in greater than 55% of cases or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 50% of expansion periods or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth indicates robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth percentiles show no significant correlation with 60-day forward returns or out-of-sample R-squared remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 275 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that trigger aggressive monetary policy tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 65% of observations above the threshold or predictive accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signals synchronized strength across labor and real estate sectors, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in greater than 55% of cases or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent main effects.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales volumes reflect immediate consumer spending strength that boosts near-term corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive skew during retail sales accelerations or rolling IC remains below 0.015 over any 3-year window.
